item.name: IOS9 v1034
item.toolTip: IOS9 is used by System Menu 1.0 and early games like Zelda: Twilight Princess, Wii Sports, Wii Play, WarioWare: Smooth Moves.

item.name: IOS12 v526
item.toolTip: IOS12 is an IOS that was previously thought to be the oldest, used by titles such as Elebits.

item.name: IOS13 v1032
item.toolTip: IOS13 is used by Photo Channel 1.0.

item.name: IOS14 v1032
item.toolTip: IOS14 is used by Pokémon Battle Revolution (Japanese version only).

item.name: IOS15 v1032
item.toolTip: IOS15 was previously used by some official channels; it has not been stubbed because the Internet Channel, which could not be updated through standard updates, previously used it.

item.name: IOS17 v1032
item.toolTip: IOS17 is used by early titles such as Bust-A-Move Bash and Super Paper Mario.

item.name: IOS21 v1039
item.toolTip: IOS21 is used by third party games (No More Heroes). many other games such as Wii Sports were also re-released under this IOS.

item.name: IOS22 v1294
item.toolTip: IOS22 is used by a few games like High School Musical: Sing It! and Boogie.

item.name: IOS28 v1807
item.toolTip: IOS28 is only used by Metroid Prime 3: Corruption. The first IOS released in modular parts.

item.name: IOS30 v2576
item.toolTip: IOS30 is used by System Menu 3.0-3.3. Officially this IOS is stubbed.

item.name: IOS31 v3608
item.toolTip: IOS31 is used by News & Weather channels. RC24 (WiiLink predecessor) used to require a patched version of IOS31 so it could download custom News & Weather data.

item.name: IOS33 v3608
item.toolTip: IOS33 is an IOS version based on IOS31 and used in games like Super Mario Galaxy.

item.name: IOS34 v3608
item.toolTip: IOS34 is used by Internet Channel v257.

item.name: IOS35 v3608
item.toolTip: IOS35 is a copy of IOS31 used by games like Call of Duty, Wii Music, etc.

item.name: IOS36 v3351
item.toolTip: IOS36 v3351 was the first IOS patched to restore the trucha bug, enabling custom IOS installation. Today it is recommended not to patch IOS36.

item.name: IOS36 v3608
item.toolTip: IOS36 v3608 is used by titles such as Super Smash Bros. Brawl and Mario Kart Wii.

item.name: IOS37 v5663
item.toolTip: IOS37 is used by several WiiWare games and older Guitar Hero/Rock Band games.

item.name: IOS38 v4124
item.toolTip: IOS38 is used by Animal Crossing: City Folk and the Nintendo Wii Speak Channel.

item.name: IOS41 v3607
item.toolTip: IOS41 is a Korea-specific IOS used by preinstalled channels. It is identical to IOS45.

item.name: IOS43 v3607
item.toolTip: IOS43 is only used by some Korean games.

item.name: IOS45 v3607
item.toolTip: IOS45 is only used by some Korean games. It is identical to IOS41.

item.name: IOS46 v3607
item.toolTip: IOS46 is an IOS used by some Korean games like Mario Kart Wii and Need for Speed Undercover.

item.name: IOS48 v4124
item.toolTip: IOS48 is used by Animal Crossing: City Folk (Korean version only).

item.name: IOS53 v5663
item.toolTip: IOS53 is used by titles such as New Super Mario Bros. Wii and Wii Fit Plus.

item.name: IOS55 v5663
item.toolTip: IOS55 is used by titles such as Wii Sports Resort and The Conduit.

item.name: IOS56 v5662
item.toolTip: IOS56 added SDHC support and is identical to IOS61. Used by Wii Speak 2.0 and the latest Shopping Channel.

item.name: IOS57 v5919
item.toolTip: IOS57 is used by many of the newer games released near the end of the production of Wii discs in 2020, although it was originally released in 2009.

item.name: IOS58 v6176
item.toolTip: IOS58 offers improved USB2 speeds and is preferred by many apps including the Homebrew Channel.

item.name: IOS59 v9249
item.toolTip: IOS59 is a Japan exclusive IOS. ModMii Wizards will only direct Japanese consoles to install it.

item.name: IOS60 v6174
item.toolTip: IOS60 is used by System Menu 4.0 & 4.1. First to support SDHC. Officially this IOS is stubbed.

item.name: IOS61 v5662
item.toolTip: IOS61 was used by the Wii Shop Channel from the System Menu 4.0-4.2 updates. Also used by Photo Channel 1.1b. It is identical to IOS56.

item.name: IOS62 v6430
item.toolTip: IOS62 is used by the Wii U Transfer Tool.

item.name: IOS70 v6687
item.toolTip: IOS70 is used by System Menu 4.2. Officially this IOS is stubbed.

item.name: IOS80 v6944
item.toolTip: IOS80 is used by System Menu 4.3.

item.name: MIOS v10
item.toolTip: MIOS is a special version of IOS that runs when the Wii enters GameCube mode.

item.name: vIOS9 v1290
item.toolTip:
This is a special version of IOS9 that only works on vWii.

While effectively this IOSs is not patched, technically it's fakesigned and re-encrypted to use the Wii retail common key so that it can be installed using WAD Managers available today.

item.toolTip: IOS50 is used by System Menu 3.4; this IOS is based on IOS60 to maximize compatibility and protection. Officially this IOS is stubbed.

item.name: IOS52[60]

item.name: IOS80[60]
item.toolTip: IOS80 is used by System Menu 4.3; this IOS is based on IOS60 to maximize compatibility and protection.

item.name: IOS236[36]
item.toolTip: IOS236 is a patched version of IOS36. It is no longer needed.

item.name: vIOS61[56]
item.toolTip: vIOS61 (aka cIOS61[56]-v5918-vWii) is a not normally found on vWii. It is a custom copy of vIOS56 with only it's slot changed to 61 to support Photo Channel 1.1.

item.name: vIOS80[80]
item.toolTip: vIOS80 is used by vWii System Menu 4.3.

item.name: 0e.app [IOS80 v6943]
item.toolTip: Used to construct NEEK for Wii.

item.toolTip: Used to construct older versions of NEEK+DI (before v186) and NEEK2O+DI (before v70) for Wii.

item.name: 0d.app [vIOS80 v7200]
item.toolTip: Used to construct NEEK for vWii.

item.name: 0c.app [MIOS v10]
item.toolTip: Used to construct DIOS MIOS for Wii.

item.toolTip: Can be installed to Wii and/or vWii.

item.name: Photo 1.0 [RF]
item.toolTip: Photo Channel v1.1 is generally an improvement over v1.0; however, v1.1 replaced MP3 support with AAC support. Users that want MP3 support may prefer v1.0.

item.toolTip: Download also includes a second WAD (Photo-Channel-1.1-dummy-HAZA) which is required for Photo Channel v1.1 to work.

item.name: EULA [U]
item.toolTip:
End User License Agreement is a hidden channel that is launched by the System Menu when the "User Agreements" option is selected, as well as by the Wii Shop Channel if the EULA has not been accepted. Because the EULA uses HTTP, a custom page can be shown there, which is exploited by str2hax and WiiLink.

While only found on the Wii, this should also get installed to vWii if enabling Priiloader's Permanent vWii System Settings (vWii) system menu hack.

item.name: EULA [E]
item.toolTip:

While only found on the Wii, this should also get installed to vWii if enabling Priiloader's Permanent vWii System Settings (vWii) system menu hack.

item.name: BC v6 [RF]
item.toolTip:
BC (short for Backwards Compatibility) is run when a GameCube game is launched from the Disc Channel. It is a bootloader similar to boot1 that is responsible for lowering clock speed, verifying and loading boot2, which detects the lowered clock speed and loads MIOS to run the GC game.

BC was never meaningfully updated other than to fix some exploits, the BC WAD is useful mainly to construct emulated NANDs.

item.name: NMM [cBC]
item.toolTip: NMM (No More Memory-Cards) is a Custom BC that redirects all GameCube Memory Card access to the SD card. This allow saving/loading GameCube game saves without a GC Memory Card.

item.name: BC-NAND vWii [RF]
item.toolTip:
BC-NAND & BC-WFS are Region Free ancast images for the vWii installed as title 1-512 & 1-513. They replace the NAND Boot Program when launching any title that includes one, and is responsible for lowering the Espresso clock speed for Wii compatibility. BC-WFS is probably only used for Dragon Quest X.

These WADs are useful mainly to construct vWii emulated NANDs.

item.name: BC-WFS vWii [RF]
item.toolTip:
BC-NAND & BC-WFS are Region Free ancast images for the vWii installed as title 1-512 & 1-513. They replace the NAND Boot Program when launching any title that includes one, and is responsible for lowering the Espresso clock speed for Wii compatibility. BC-WFS is probably only used for Dragon Quest X.

These WADs are useful mainly to construct vWii emulated NANDs.

item.name: Bannerbomb v1 [3.0-4.1]
item.toolTip: <=2GB SD Card required; larger SDHC and SDXC also work if on System Menu 4.0 or 4.1; can launch both dols and elfs; works on Wii only

item.name: Bannerbomb v2 [4.2]
item.toolTip: SD Card required; SDHC and SDXC also work; can launch both dols and elfs; works on Wii only

item.name: Wilbrand
item.toolTip:
MailBox Bomb similar to Letterbomb; technically also works on lower System Menus (see https://wilbrand.donut.eu.org) but ModMii uses Bannerbomb for that.

SD Card required; SDHC and SDXC also work if on System Menu 4.0 or higher; Can launch both dols and elfs; works on Wii only

item.name: Smash Stack
item.toolTip: Requires Super Smash Brothers Brawl and a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work); can launch elfs but not dols; works on Wii and vWii

item.name: Bathaxx [U/E/J]
item.toolTip: Requires LEGO Batman, a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work) and a nunchuck; can launch elfs but not dols; works on Wii and vWii

item.name: Eri HaKawai [U/E/J]
item.toolTip: Requires Tales of Symphonia: Dawn of the New World, a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work) and a nunchuck; can launch elfs but not dols; works on Wii and vWii

item.name: Indiana Pwns [U/E/J]
item.toolTip: Requires LEGO Indiana Jones, a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work) and a nunchuck; can launch elfs but not dols; works on Wii and vWii

item.name: Return of the Jodi [U/E/J]
item.toolTip: Requires LEGO Star Wars, a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work) and a nunchuck; can launch elfs but not dols; works on Wii and vWii

item.name: YU-GI-OWNED! [U/E/J]
item.toolTip: Requires Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D, a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work) and a nunchuck or classic controller; can launch elfs but not dols; works on Wii and vWii

item.name: Twilight Hack 1 [<3.4 U/E/J]
item.toolTip:
Version 0.1beta1 is compatible with System Menus up to 3.3, 0.1beta2 is compatible with System Menu 3.4.

Requires The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess, a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work) and a nunchuck; can launch both dols and elfs; works on Wii only

item.name: Twilight Hack 2 [3.4 U/E/J]
item.toolTip:
Version 0.1beta1 is compatible with System Menus up to 3.3, 0.1beta2 is compatible with System Menu 3.4.

Requires The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess, a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work) and a nunchuck; can launch both dols and elfs; works on Wii only

item.name: BlueMii
item.toolTip: A BlueBomb-based exploit for the Wii or Wii Mini requiring a separate already-hacked Wii / WiiU (vWii) / Wii Mini or a copy of Dolphin with working Bluetooth passthrough.

item.name: BlueBomb
item.toolTip:
An exploit for the Wii or Wii Mini requiring a bluetooth adapter and a linux machine (or Raspberry Pi, or Raspberry Pi Pico W 1/2, or ESP32 with BR/EDR [Classic] support) and is therefore not directly supported by ModMii; however, Linux can still be run on a Windows PC using software like VirtualBox or by flashing a Linux Live environment to a USB flash drive.

Download is not available directly via ModMii.

item.name: szsHaxx
item.toolTip:
Requires Mario Kart Wii, a <=2GB SD card (SDHC and SDXC do not work), deleting savegame without being able to back it up; works on Wii only

Download is not available directly via ModMii due to restrictive requirements.

item.name: BootMii SD Files
item.toolTip: A BootMii folder that when saved to SD can be used to launch BootMii; BootMii can also be retrieved from the HackMii Installer.

item.name: ARC ModMii Edition
item.toolTip: Any Region Changer (ARC) ModMii Edition is a mod of ARC for the Wii. If it detects a region mismatch, you only have to select the "Auto Fix Settings" to correct it. It is one of many tools used to complete a region change.

item.name: KoreanKii
item.toolTip:
Wii app to add or remove the Korean key from the SEEPROM. Used when region changing to and from Korean System Menu Versions.

WARNING: You will 003 brick if you install the Korean Key UNLESS you are on a Korean System Menu version and/or have IOS60 installed as your System Menu IOS.

item.name: nSwitch Channel
item.toolTip:
Simple neek/realnand switcher app embedded in a channel (neek2o_ModMii_BK2O_nSwitch_v4.2.wad).

Technically also works on vWii if neek is set up properly.

item.name: Switch2Uneek
item.toolTip: An app to switch to and from UNEEK and back; download also includes a channel/WAD.

item.name: Casper
item.toolTip: A tool to run MINI apps like BootMii or SNEEK on your Wii without having to make permanent mods to it.

item.name: IOS236 Installer
item.toolTip: Deprecated softmod tool that installs a patched IOS36 in IOS slot 236. Requires HBC version 1.0.7 or higher.

item.name: Simple IOS Patcher
item.toolTip: Deprecated softmod tool that can install patched IOS36, 60, 70 and 80 to various slots. Requires HBC version 1.0.7 or higher.

item.name: ww-43db-patcher
item.toolTip:
Patches the WiiWare 4:3 aspect ratio database (43DB) within vWii's System Menu U8 archive to remove WiiConnect24-related channel entries (Everybody Votes Channel, Check Mii Out Channel) to enable a 16:9 aspect ratio.

Redundant if any vWii theme built using ModMii has been installed (even the Default theme).

item.name: Patched IOS80 Installer
item.toolTip: Patched IOS80 Installer for vWii installs a patched vWii IOS80. Made redundant by the Fakesigned vIOS80[80] WAD from Download page 1.

item.name: HBC v1.1.3 WAD [OHBC]
item.toolTip: Open Homebrew Channel v1.1.3 WAD by fail0verflow with title ID OHBC. This is the same channel intalled by vWii Compat Installer v1.6. Technically it works on both Wii & vWii but is only recommended for vWii.

item.name: HBC v1.1.4-1 WAD [OHBC]
item.toolTip: Open Homebrew Channel v1.1.4-1 WAD modified by FIX94 with title ID OHBC. Technically it works on both Wii & vWii.

item.name: HBC v1.1.5 WAD [LULZ]
item.toolTip: Open Homebrew Channel v1.1.5 WAD with title ID LULZ; this is the same as fail0verflow's v1.1.3 but with it's Title ID changed to LULZ. Technically it works on both Wii & vWii but is only recommended for Wii.

item.name: OSC Wii App Library
altnames: Open Shop Channel Wii App Library
item.toolTip:
Download all 200+ (v)Wii apps available via the Open Shop Channel: https://oscwii.org/library

Apps can be excluded from this download by configuring ModMii's "Skipped Apps" Option.

item.name: Homebrew Browser
item.toolTip: Download homebrew apps (selection is the same as the Open Shop Channel) using your (v)Wii.

item.name: HackMii Installer v1.2
item.toolTip: Used to install the Homebrew Channel v1.1.2 and BootMii 1.5.

item.name: HackMii Installer v1.0
item.toolTip: Used to install the Homebrew Channel v1.1.0 and BootMii 1.4.

item.name: Priiloader
item.toolTip:
Priiloader adds a level of brick protection to your (v)Wii.
It loads before the System Menu, can enable system menu hacks and be set to autoboot the HBC, BootMii, or whatever homebrew you want!
Checking this box will also download the Load Priiloader app.

Note Priiloader can also update itself.

item.name: nanddumper@IOS
item.toolTip: An alternative tool to create a BootMii compatible nand.bin and keys.bin backup saved to an SD Card or USB Hard Drive.

item.name: SysCheck ModMii Edition
item.toolTip: SysCheck ModMii Edition retrieves information about what is installed on your Wii/vWii.

item.name: YAWM ModMii Edition
altnames: Yet Another WAD Manager ModMii Edition
item.toolTip:
Yet Another WAD Manager ModMii Edition allows you to (un)install WADs and launch apps.
It has built in protection to prevent actions that would cause brick, the ability to install a new System Menu without losing Priiloader, and more!

item.name: Multi-Mod Manager
item.toolTip: MMM is a multi-purpose tool for (v)Wii that can install WADs, launch apps, etc, but does not support Motion Plus wiimotes.

item.name: System Channel Restorer
item.toolTip:
Easily re-install missing/removed system apps, e.g. Mii Channel, Photo Channel, etc.

Technically also works on Wii Mini.

item.name: csm-installer
item.toolTip:
A (v)Wii App that installs System Menu themes from CSM files created by ModMii, ThemeMii or MyMenu.
Also supports APP files to restore original themes. Drag a mym file onto ModMii to convert it to csm.

WARNING: Make sure you have Priiloader, BootMii and a NAND backup before experimenting with themes!

item.name: MyMenuifyMod
item.toolTip:
WARNING: Do not use MyMenuifyMod v2.0 or older on vWii! Use the latest version or csm-installer instead.

Installs System Menu themes from CSM files created by ModMii, ThemeMii or MyMenu.
Also supports APP files to restore original themes.
Drag a mym file onto ModMii to convert it to csm.

WARNING: Make sure you have Priiloader, BootMii and a NAND backup before experimenting with themes!

item.name: WiiXplorer
item.toolTip: File explorer for the (v)Wii with several other features.

item.name: SaveGame Manager GX
item.toolTip: Extract/install SaveGames and Miis from/to your (v)Wii.

item.name: Locked HBC Folder
item.toolTip:
A password protected apps_locked folder you can move (v)Wii homebrew apps to restrict access to them.

Default Password: UDLRAB

item.name: CleanRip
item.toolTip:
A tool to backup your Gamecube/Wii Discs via IOS58.
Create 1:1 backups of your GC/Wii discs for archival purposes without any requirements for custom IOS (cIOS).
Supports USB 2.0 / NTFS* / FAT32 & Front SD. Devices formatted as WBFS is not supported.

* Warning: ripping games to NTFS is unstable, it is highly recommended to rip games to a FAT32 formatted HDD, then transfer them to your NTFS drive using a computer.

item.name: ftpii
item.toolTip: FTP Server for the (v)Wii; use it with FileZilla to manage files on your (v)Wii using your PC.

item.name: USB Loader GX
item.toolTip:
A popular USB-Loader with lots of extra features.

Note this app can also update itself.

item.name: USB-Loader Channel/dol
item.toolTip:
Multi Forwarder Channel WAD for Wii & vWii.
forwards to GX, WiiFlow, CFG, and more!
The bundled dol file can be installed to Priiloader to autoboot the channel.

item.name: WiiFlow Lite
item.toolTip: A popular USB-Loader known for attractive visual effects.

item.name: WiiFlow Channel/dol
item.toolTip: WiiFlow forwarder channel for Wii & vWii which can be installed using a WAD Manager. The bundled dol file can be installed to Priiloader to autoboot the channel.

item.name: CFG USB Loader
item.toolTip: Configurable USB Loader is a popular USB-Loader that is highly customizable.

item.name: Nintendont
item.toolTip: Gamecube backup loader; Also works as a Gamecube plugin with other USB-Loaders.

item.name: postLoader
item.toolTip:
All in one alternative to the System Menu, HBC and/or USB-Loaders.
Can launch Wii games, GameCube games, VC/Wiiware titles from real or emulated NAND, and can also boot fceugx, vbagx, snes9xgx, and genplusgx games.

item.name: postLoader Channel
item.toolTip: postLoader forwarder channel for Wii & vWii which can be installed using a WAD Manager.

item.name: Neogamma
item.toolTip: Backup Disc Loader also able to load from SD and USB.

item.name: WiiMC-SS
item.toolTip: Wii Media Center SuperrSonic Edition

item.name: FCEUGX
item.toolTip: NES Emulator

item.name: SNES9xGX
item.toolTip: SNES Emulator

item.name: Visual Boy Advance GX
item.toolTip: GBA/GBC/GB Emulator

item.name: Wii64
item.toolTip: N64 Emulator

item.name: Not64
item.toolTip: N64 Emulator

item.name: WiiSXRX
item.toolTip: PS1 Emulator

item.name: Cheat Codes
item.toolTip: txtcodes folder filled with cheat codes.

item.name: AccioHacks
item.toolTip:
Cheat Code Manager

to convert txtcodes to an AccioHacks compatible format toggle this download at the same time as "Cheat Codes" (above), or load the ModMii Download Queue called CheatCodes_For_AccioHacks.

item.name: WiiMedic
item.toolTip: A comprehensive diagnostic and health monitoring tool for (v)Wii.

item.name: Hermes-v5.1 cIOS202 [60]
item.toolTip: Semi-Recommended cIOS. It is the ideal cIOS for slot 202 however it is outdated: cIOS202 was used for older versions of some popular emulators.

item.name: Hermes-v5.1 cIOS222 [38]
item.toolTip: Since d2x, Hermes cIOSs are generally no longer needed.

item.name: Hermes-v5.1 cIOS223 [37]
item.toolTip: Since d2x, Hermes cIOSs are generally no longer needed.

item.name: Hermes-v5.1 cIOS224 [57]
item.toolTip: Semi-Recommended cIOS. It is the ideal cIOS for slot 224 however it is outdated. Since d2x, Hermes cIOSs are generally no longer needed.

item.name: Hermes-v5 cIOS222 [38]
item.toolTip: Since d2x, Hermes cIOSs are generally no longer needed.

item.name: Hermes-v5 cIOS223 [37]
item.toolTip: Since d2x, Hermes cIOSs are generally no longer needed.

item.name: Hermes-v5 cIOS224 [57]
item.toolTip: Since d2x, Hermes cIOSs are generally no longer needed.

item.name: Hermes-v4 cIOS222 [38]
item.toolTip: Semi-Recommended cIOS. It is the ideal cIOS for slot 222 however it is outdated. Since d2x, Hermes cIOSs are generally no longer needed.

item.name: Hermes-v4 cIOS223 [37+38]
item.toolTip: Semi-Recommended cIOS. It is the ideal cIOS for slot 223 and was widely used for compatibility with guitar-hero games however it is outdated. Since d2x, Hermes cIOSs are generally no longer needed.

item.name: Swiss cMIOS r1788 [10]
item.toolTip:
Semi-Recommended cMIOS. A cMIOS with Swiss r1788 built-in. Hold Y during any GameCube mode load to start the built-in Swiss.  If Y is not held then it should behave identically to WiiGator/WiiPower's cMIOS and official MIOS. While this is the ideal cMIOS, because of Nintendont, cMIOSs are generally not needed unless loading backup Gamecube discs on older compatible Wiis.

Based on MIOSv10.

item.name: WiiGator/Power cMIOS-v0.2 [10]
item.toolTip:
Since Nintendont, cMIOSs are generally not needed unless loading backup Gamecube discs on older compatible Wiis.

Based on MIOSv10.

item.name: WiiGator cMIOS-v0.2 [4]
item.toolTip:
Since Nintendont, cMIOSs are generally not needed unless loading backup Gamecube discs on older compatible Wiis.

Based on MIOSv4.

item.name: Waninkoko cMIOS-rev5 [4]
item.toolTip:
Since Nintendont, cMIOSs are generally not needed unless loading backup Gamecube discs on older compatible Wiis.

Based on MIOSv4.

item.name: DIOS MIOS Lite (DML) v2.11
item.toolTip:
Since Nintendont, DML is generally not used anymore.

DML (Dios Mios Lite) is a tool which allows you to run Gamecube games from an SD Card.

Note: for DML to work with SNEEK+DI the WAD must be installed to real NAND and accessed via an emulated NAND.

Games must be in discex format, use discex or ModMii's Game Bulk Extractor.

item.name: DIOS MIOS v2.11
item.toolTip:
Since Nintendont, DIOS MIOS is generally not used anymore.

DIOS MIOS is a tool which allows you to run Gamecube games from a USB.

Games must be in discex format, use discex or ModMii's Game Bulk Extractor.

item.name: FAT32 GUI Formatter
item.toolTip: A tool that can format large SD cards and hard drives as FAT32.

item.name: TinyWiiBackupManager
item.toolTip: Manage Wii/GameCube games, Wii Homebrew apps, covers and cheats. All major formats supported including wbfs, rvz, iso, etc.

item.name: Wii Backup Manager
item.toolTip: Manage Wii backups on your SD card or hard drive and more. Use this tool to convert games to WBFS files (does not support Nkit ISOs).

item.name: NKit Processing App
item.toolTip:
A Nintendo ToolKit that can Recover and Preserve Wii and GameCube disc images.

If you have an NKit ISO use the NKit Processing App to convert to regular ISO, then use Wii Backup Manager to convert to WBFS.

item.name: ShowMiiWads
item.toolTip: A "WAD File Manager" for your computer that displays information about WAD files, edit them, install or remove them from Emulated NANDs, and more!

item.name: CustomizeMii
item.toolTip:
A custom channel creator for the Wii.

ModMii's forwarder dols from the Advanced Downloads Page are better and easier to construct than Customize Mii's; simply build a forwarder dol using ModMii then add the new dol to your channel using Customize Mii's Options tab.

If ahbprot is needed for your channel make sure to use a base WAD that has it already enabled (e.g. ModMii's USB-Loader Channel).

item.name: ThemeMii Mod
item.toolTip:
Wii Theme Builder GUI capable of converting csm's to mym's and vice versa. Install themes using csm-installer.

WARNING: Make sure you have Priiloader, BootMii and a NAND backup before experimenting with themes!

Note: currently this tool cannot download Base App files, these can instead be retrieved from ModMii's Download Page 3.

item.name: Wii Game Shortcut Creator
item.toolTip: WiiGSC (formerly Crap) creates shortcut channels to specific Wii games so they can be loaded from the Wii/vWii menu instead of a USB loader.

item.name: Ohneschwanzenegger
item.toolTip: aka NUS NANDBuilder; create, format, & write to a nand.bin that can be installed with BootMii or a flash programmer.

item.name: UStealth
item.toolTip:
With UStealth you can hide USB hard drives so that the WiiU no longer asks you to Format them. The disk is then also hidden from your computer. If you want to copy something onto it later you have to make your device visible again using UStealth or directly on the console using 'USB Toggle 2.0'. Of course, you should not format it if your computer asks you to!

Not needed if Aroma's 'Avoid Format dialog' is enabled.

item.name: H2testw
item.toolTip: SD Card and USB Test Tool

item.name: Open Shop Channel
item.toolTip:
A tool to download even more homebrew apps using your PC (selection is the same as the Homebrew Browser).

Note this app can also update itself.

item.name: GameCube Backup Manager
item.toolTip:
Manage and convert Gamecube backup files; requires a 64-bit OS (32-bit users can google for 'Gamecube ISO Tool').

Note this app can also update itself.

item.name: WiiLink Patcher GUI
item.toolTip:
WiiLink Patcher GUI helps you install WiiLink to bring back WiiConnect24 features to your (v)Wii.

Note this app can also detect when there's updates.

item.name: NUSGet
item.toolTip:
A modern and supercharged NUS downloader that can download content from Nintendo Update Servers.

Note this app can also update itself.

item.name: Dolphin
item.toolTip:
Wii / Gamecube Emulator; requires 64-bit Windows 10/macOS 11 or newer (older versions of Dolphin from their website support other operating systems).

Note this app can also update itself.

item.name: Cemu
item.toolTip:
WiiU Emulator; requires a 64-bit OS.

Note this app can also update itself.

item.name: Diskitude
item.toolTip: Disk Usage Analyzer

item.name: FileZilla
item.toolTip:
FTP Client; use it with ftpii or FTPiiU to manage files on your (v)Wii or Wii U.

Note this app can also update itself.

item.name: HashMyFiles
item.toolTip: Calculates and compares file hashes, can also be launched from Windows Explorer context menu.

item.name: HxD Hex Editor
item.toolTip: Note this app can also update itself.

item.name: UWUVCI
item.toolTip:
Custom build of Ultimate Wii U Virtual Console Injectors (UWUVCI), which is an all-in-one injector for the WiiU.

Supported systems: Wii, GCN, DS, GBA, N64, SNES, NES, Turbo Grafx, MSX

item.name: Homebrew App Store WiiU Library
altnames: Homebrew App Store Wii U Library
HB App Store Wii U Library
item.toolTip:
Download all 150+ WiiU homebrew available via the Homebrew App Store: https://hb-app.store/wiiu

Apps can be excluded from this download by configuring ModMii's 'Skipped Apps' Option.

item.name: Homebrew App Store
item.toolTip:
Download homebrew apps using your WiiU (selection is the same as hb-app.store).
Compatible with Aroma, Tiramisu and Legacy Environments.

item.name: Aroma
item.toolTip: Aroma is the recommended homebrew environment for the WiiU.

item.name: NAND Dumper
item.toolTip: Create backups of the WiiU's NAND.

item.name: Environment Loader
item.toolTip: Environment Loader allows you load environments on your WiiU. Examples of WiiU environments are Aroma and Tiramisu.

item.name: vWii Compat Installer
item.toolTip:
Installs the Homebrew Channel to the vWii Menu from WiiU Mode.
Compatible with Aroma and Tiramisu Environments.

item.name: Wii U Disc Dumper
item.toolTip:
Wii U Disc Dumper (WUDD); supports dumping to SD (FAT32) and USB (NTFS only).
Compatible with Aroma and Tiramisu Environments.

item.name: WUP Installer GX2
item.toolTip:
WUP Installer GX2 (WUHB); can install public titles such as games, game updates or DLC to your system memory (NAND) or the WiiU formatted USB.
Compatible with Aroma Environment.

item.name: Wii U Account Swap
item.toolTip:
A tool to manage a user's account.dat, particularly for Network IDs.
Compatible with Aroma.

item.name: Wiiload Plugin
item.toolTip: An Aroma plugin intended for developers to load homebrew and plugins via the network.

item.name: Bloopair
item.toolTip:
Allows connecting controllers from other consoles like native WiiU Pro Controllers on the WiiU.
Compatible with Aroma and Tiramisu Environments.

item.name: Inkay
item.toolTip: An Aroma plugin that enables the Pretendo Network; a free and open source replacement for Nintendo's servers for both the 3DS and WiiU, allowing online connectivity even after the original servers have been discontinued.

item.name: GiveMiiYouTube
item.toolTip: An Aroma plugin that allows the WiiU YouTube app to continue functioning after its discontinuation.

item.name: Screenshot
item.toolTip: An Aroma plugin that takes screenshots of the TV and GamePad screen.

item.name: Enhanced vWii
item.toolTip: An Aroma plugin to enhance the vWii mode.

item.name: Wii VC Launch
item.toolTip: An Aroma plugin to enhance launching Wii Virtual Console titles.

item.name: SaveMii Mod WUT Port
item.toolTip:
WiiU/vWii Save Manager.
Compatible with Aroma and Tiramisu Environments.

item.name: WiiUIdent
item.toolTip:
WiiUIdent; A WiiU Identification homebrew, which display information about console hardware components, software/hardware versions and much more.
Compatible with Aroma and Tiramisu Environments.

item.name: FTPiiU Plugin
item.toolTip: An FTP server plugin for Aroma.

item.name: Tiramisu
item.toolTip: Tiramisu is an alternative homebrew environment for the WiiU meant to run Legacy homebrew that was popular before the development of Aroma.
